user.title: Add an AirVisual Outdoor
user.description: Polls IQAir's public node API for your own AirVisual Outdoor unit. No account or API key needed — the unit must be published as a station.

data_description.node_id: The unit's 24-character hex ID, e.g. from your IQAir dashboard URL.
data_description.name: Defaults to the name the unit reports (usually "AirVisual Outdoor").
data_description.aqi_scale: Which AQI standard the AQI and main-pollutant sensors report. One scale per entry — reconfigure later to switch.
data_description.mac: If set, Home Assistant merges this device with its network (DHCP/UniFi) client entry.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure AirVisual Outdoor
reconfigure.description: Node {node_id}. The node ID itself cannot change — a different unit is a new entry.

data_description.aqi_scale: Switching scale creates fresh AQI and main-pollutant entities under the new scale.
data_description.mac: If set, Home Assistant merges this device with its network (DHCP/UniFi) client entry.

error.invalid_node_id: Node IDs are exactly 24 hex characters (0-9, a-f).
error.invalid_mac: That doesn't look like a MAC address (AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF).
error.device_not_found: IQAir's API has no device with this node ID.
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach IQAir's node API. Check connectivity and try again.

update_failed.message: Error talking to the IQAir node API: {error}

rate_limited.message: The node's hourly request budget is exhausted; it resets at the top of the hour.
